title: Parallel scheduler hardening
goal: Make parallel worker execution release-safe by collecting all worker outcomes deterministically, preserving artifacts, and making failure/cancellation behavior explicit before v0.1.0.
acceptance: Parallel worker batches always join every spawned worker thread before returning success or failure; no worker thread is dropped/detached because an earlier sibling failed.

If any worker in a parallel layer fails, Khazad-Doom records deterministic per-slice outcomes for all workers in that layer that reached a terminal result, then fails or blocks the run with a clear summary.
Cancellation during a parallel layer is graceful-first and propagates to all active workers before the layer returns.
Monitor/status output clearly identifies a parallel worker layer and the active slices in that layer while workers are running.
Tests cover at least one parallel layer where one worker fails while another succeeds or is still running, proving the daemon joins/records outcomes rather than abandoning handles.
No integration merges happen until the entire parallel layer has completed successfully; integration remains serial and deterministic.
must_ask_if: The implementation would merge a successful worker from a layer after a sibling failed without an explicit policy decision.
The implementation would abandon or detach worker threads/processes after the first parallel failure.
The implementation would weaken worker attempt history or artifact preservation.
